title: Weird graphical issues between versions..
author: Kvarkas
body_snippet:
### Discussed in https://github.com/orgs/mRemoteNG/discussions/2798

<div type='discussions-op-text'>

<sup>Originally posted by **neilpeel13** September 16, 2025</sup>
Hi there
Quick discussion first, to see if this is in fact a bug..

Basically, I have a new work laptop and when using the new version 1.78.2 (any of them) also 1.78.1 - when connecting to RDP session, the graphics are a bit blurry and some of the app screens don't display as expected.

Installed 1.77.2 (.net6 version) and ...

iterations.description: Marked Cannot Reproduce with legacy 1.78.x labels; a DPI/scaling workaround exists but root cause on current .NET 10 build is unconfirmed and requires reporter to retest on latest fork build with reproducible steps.
Wontfix: DPI awareness is already set to PerMonitorV2 in .NET 10 — specific blurriness needs reproduction steps

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: For the reference within this issue and not only in the discussion: The issue appears to be independent on the selected RDP Version.

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: @neilpeel13: What's your scaling settings within Windows itself? And what Windows Version are you running on?

comments.author: TimChaubet-I4U
comments.snippet:
Workaround for the blurriness with mRemoteNG and windows 11: 

- Rightclick mRemoteNG.exe and go to properties
- Compatibility tab
- Change high DPI settings
- Check "Use this setting to fix scaling problems ..." and select in the "Use the DPI that's set for my main display when" to "I signed in to Windows"
- Check "override high DPI scaling behaviour" and set it to system

This way it won't rescale the truetype fonts and your menu & ssh session fonts will no longer be blurry.
Perhaps the devs c...

notes: Graphical issues between versions. Version-specific rendering, not actionable.
